frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'Behind the Knife' branding
Equipment list slide showing required materials for two-handed tie practice
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows knot tying board, practice string/suture, and gloves as equipment

frame_analyses.description: Hands positioned to demonstrate two-handed tie technique with suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial hand positioning for left-hand dominant tie demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Demonstration of crossing suture ends to form initial throw configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper crossing technique for establishing throw direction

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating suture through throw completion with finger rotation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates finger positioning and rotation for securing throw

frame_analyses.description: Review slide with detailed text instructions for L-formation and crossing technique

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional video demonstrating two-handed tie technique, not a performance to be scored
technique_summary: Educational demonstration of two-handed tie technique for laparoscopic suturing skills development

demonstrated_techniques: two-handed tie formation using L-shaped hand positioning
proper suture crossing technique to form X configuration
finger rotation and pinching method for securing throws
verbal_cues: Start by making L's with both hands and drape suture around index fingers
Cross right side of suture over left index finger to make an X
Pinch index finger and thumb together and rotate
Make C's with both hands and suture draped around thumbs
common_errors_addressed: Failure to maintain proper finger positioning during rotation
